name: Southern First Bank
description_short: Southern First Bank is a relationship-driven community bank headquartered in Greenville, SC with $4B in assets and 13 locations across the Southeast, offering personal and business banking services.
description_long:
Southern First Bancshares, Inc. (NASDAQ: SFST) operates Southern First Bank, a regional bank founded on a relationship-first philosophy. The bank is headquartered in Greenville, South Carolina and maintains 13 locations throughout the Southeast, including branches in Atlanta, Charleston, Charlotte, Columbia, Greensboro, Greenville, Raleigh, and Summerville. As of Q4 2025, the bank reported $4 billion in total assets and emphasized local ownership and leadership.

Southern First offers comprehensive personal and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, online banking with mobile app access, personal service from dedicated banker teams, and loan products. The bank features digital tools like Zelle for peer-to-peer money transfers, online account management, and digital enrollment options. Both personal and business customers can access online banking portals with username/password authentication and dedicated support lines (864-679-9000 for business, 877-679-9646 for personal accounts).

The bank distinguishes itself through a stated "relationship-first" approach where each account comes with a dedicated team of bankers focused on understanding customer goals. The company emphasizes personal service and easy-to-use technology as core differentiators. Recent financial performance (Q4 2025) highlighted solid loan growth, strong deposit growth, expanding net interest margins (up 36 basis points year-over-year), and what management described as "outstanding asset quality." The bank actively participates in community initiatives, receiving recognition from the Charleston Mayor for National Homeownership Month in June 2025.

As a regional community bank, Southern First serves customers in growing Southeast markets. The relationship-driven model appeals to those seeking personalized banking rather than large national institutions. However, with only 13 locations, geographic reach is limited compared to national banks. The bank's size and regional focus position it between large national banks and smaller community banks, offering both local decision-making and adequate resources. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking portal for personal accounts
Online banking portal for business accounts
Mobile banking app with Zelle integration for peer-to-peer transfers
Dedicated banker teams for account management and financial planning
Password reset and account recovery services
Quarterly earnings and investor relations reporting
pros: Dedicated banker teams assigned to each account for personalized service and relationship-based financial planning
Strong recent financial performance with $4 billion in assets and Q4 2025 showing 36 basis points expansion in net interest margin year-over-year
Multiple Southeast locations (13 branches) across growing markets including Atlanta, Charleston, Charlotte, Columbia, Greensboro, Greenville, Raleigh, and Summerville
Digital banking conveniences including mobile app, Zelle money transfer integration, and online account management
NASDAQ-listed public company (SFST) with transparent quarterly earnings reports and investor relations materials
Community engagement with recognition for financial literacy and homeownership initiatives
Local ownership and leadership structure allowing for regional decision-making
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only 13 locations across Southeast, not suitable for customers needing nationwide branch access
As a regional bank, may have higher fees or less competitive rates on some products compared to larger national institutions
Website content does not specify APRs, fees, minimum balances, or rate details for checking, savings, or loan products
No mention of specialized products like investment services, wealth management, or insurance offerings
Limited information about business banking specific features or commercial lending products on public-facing website
best_for: Southeast residents (SC, GA, NC) seeking personalized banking with dedicated banker relationships over impersonal online-only banks
Small business owners in the Southeast who value local decision-making and relationship-based lending
High-net-worth individuals in Southern First's service areas who prioritize personal service and customized financial planning
Customers relocating to Southeast markets who want to establish banking relationships with roots in their new communities
diagnosis: Southern First Bank is best for Southeast residents and small business owners who prioritize personalized banker relationships and local decision-making over the convenience of nationwide branch networks. The primary caveat is limited geographic reach—this bank only serves 13 locations in the Southeast, making it unsuitable for customers needing multistate banking or frequent out-of-region access.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Southern First Bank in Greenville, SC offers personal and business banking across 13 Southeast locations with $4B in assets.
